rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; total_hits>=1 => FAIL

results.reason: Source-domain page; no content-word phrase substring; sem 0.20<0.7; func 0.04<0.7.
Source-domain tourism page; no phrase match; sem/func well below 0.7.
Paleoenvironmental baray paper; source-domain only; sem/func below threshold.
Mid-training survey; no traffic-synced wet/dry phase split; sem 0.58, func 0.55 both <0.7.
Periodic async RL workflow, not traffic-synced training cycle; both metrics just under 0.7.
LR-schedule paper; no phase ingest/distribute split; sem/func below threshold.
Cache eviction policy, not gradient-reservoir buffered training; sem/func below 0.7.
STAR traffic-pattern-aware decode-phase rescheduling between hot/cold queries captures traffic-synced phase-cycle effect; sem=0.70 meets threshold, func=0.72>=0.7.

fail_reason: Result 8 (STAR Decode-Phase Rescheduling, arXiv 2510.13668v2) hits both semantic (0.70>=0.7) and functional (0.72>=0.7) thresholds; its traffic-pattern-aware phase rescheduling between hot/cold query windows matches the BBPLTC traffic-synced wet-collect/dry-distribute phase-cycle effect cluster, defeating the novelty claim.
